text.title: Sorbet Strict Floor for Agent-Authored Files
text.summary: Files in the agent's edit surface declare `# typed: true` minimum, with `srb tc` blocking PRs on any typecheck failure.

section.title: Agent-edit files MUST be at least typed:true
section.p: Sorbet's sigil controls how strictly each file is checked. `false` runs only constant resolution; `true` checks method calls and signatures; `strict` additionally forbids `T.untyped` returns and untyped instance variables. Files an agent may modify autonomously must hit at least `true` so the typechecker can refuse hallucinated calls.

rule.statement: Every Ruby file under directories writable by an AI agent MUST start with `# typed: true` or `# typed: strict`. CI MUST run `bundle exec srb tc` and `bundle exec tapioca check-shims`; both must exit 0 before merge. Agents are forbidden from downgrading a file's sigil to silence errors; they must fix the type instead.
rule.rationale: Sorbet at `typed: true` rejects calls to methods Sorbet does not see in any RBI, which is exactly the failure mode of LLM-invented methods. Without `srb tc` in CI, the typecheck is advisory and agents will route around it (per Shopify's adoption write-up).

example.note: Explicit `sig` blocks; strict sigil; no `T.untyped` returns.

example.code: # typed: false # downgraded by agent to silence errors class UserMailer def self.welcome(user) Mail.new(to: user.emial, subject: "Welcome") # typo, runtime crash end end
example.note: Sigil downgrade hides the typo until production; the floor is gone.

antipattern.description: Agent appends `T.unsafe(self).method_name(...)` to bypass a Sorbet error instead of fixing the type or generating the missing RBI.
antipattern.reason: `T.unsafe` blackholes type information past that point, defeating the gate; legitimate uses are rare and require human review with a comment.

section.reference: Sorbet path config with strict-only directories.
